title: Secondary Process Character Mnemonics
text: Secondary Process Character Mnemonics These mnemonics define the character used in a secondary process, for example, for printers which must print a line twice to produce an underline. This technique is also used with some display devices. Arguments Mnemonic Default Explanation printer_underscore _ Character used with _underscore. printer_inverse . Character used with _inverse. Description If the printer_inverse mnemonic is not defined, only spaces in inverse text are filled with a period (.). If the printer_inverse mnemonic is defined, the specified character is printed over each inverse position. If you define any of the color mnemonics (that is, color, color_n, _color , or _color_ n ) in the device section, any definition for _inverse is ignored. This also means that printer_inverse is ignored. The following example shows the definition used in the standard HP2645 translation table: ^BEGIN_SET 0 ^NUL ^SELECT ... _ ^PRINTER_UNDERSCORE ... ^END_SET Copy Related Links Uniface Index Color Mnemonics Color mnemonics in the table define the 64 Uniface index color combinations. Secondary Process Color and Video Mnemonics The secondary process mnemonics instruct Uniface to perform the video feature in a secondary print or display process, that is, by printing the line a second time.
